Package calendar.model

Class CalendarEvent

  • java.lang.Object
    • calendar.model.CalendarEvent
  • public final class CalendarEvent
extends java.lang.Object
    Immutable representation of a calendar event with support for both single and recurring occurrences.

    This class is fully immutable - all fields are final and any mutable data (arrays) are defensively copied. Use the Builder pattern to create instances with optional fields.

    A unified representation where:

    • Single events have seriesId = null
    • Recurring events have seriesId != null (UUID)

    Uniqueness is defined by the tuple (subject, start, end).

    • Nested Class Summary

      Builder class for constructing CalendarEvent instances.

    • Method Detail

      • builder

        public static CalendarEvent.Builder builder​(java.lang.String subject,
                                            java.time.LocalDateTime start,
                                            java.time.LocalDateTime end)
        Creates a builder for a single (non-recurring) event.
        Parameters:
        subject - the event subject (required, non-empty)
        start - the start date-time (required)
        end - the end date-time (required, must be after start)
        Returns:
        a new Builder instance
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if validation fails

      • recurringBuilder

        public static CalendarEvent.Builder recurringBuilder​(java.lang.String subject,
                                                     java.time.LocalDateTime start,
                                                     java.time.LocalDateTime end,
                                                     Weekday[] recurrenceDays)
        Creates a builder for a recurring event.
        Parameters:
        subject - the event subject
        start - the start date-time
        end - the end date-time
        recurrenceDays - days of the week to repeat on
        Returns:
        a new Builder instance for recurring events
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if validation fails

      • from

        public static CalendarEvent.Builder from​(CalendarEvent event)
        Creates a copy builder from an existing event. Useful for creating modified versions of immutable events.
        Parameters:
        event - the event to copy
        Returns:
        a new Builder with all fields copied from the event

        public java.lang.String getSubject()

        public java.time.LocalDateTime getStart()

        public EventStatus getStatus()

      • getSeriesId

        public java.lang.String getSeriesId()

      • getRecurrenceDays

        public Weekday[] getRecurrenceDays()
        Returns a defensive copy of recurrence days array.
        Returns:
        copy of recurrence days array, or null if not recurring

      • getRepeatCount

        public java.lang.Integer getRepeatCount()

      • getRepeatUntil

        public java.time.LocalDateTime getRepeatUntil()

      • isRecurring

        public boolean isRecurring()
        Checks if this is a recurring event.
        Returns:
        true if part of a series, false if single event

      • withDescription

        public CalendarEvent withDescription​(java.lang.String newDescription)
        Creates a new CalendarEvent with updated description.
        Parameters:
        newDescription - the new description
        Returns:
        a new CalendarEvent instance with the updated description

      • withLocation

        public CalendarEvent withLocation​(java.lang.String newLocation)
        Creates a new CalendarEvent with updated location.
        Parameters:
        newLocation - the new location
        Returns:
        a new CalendarEvent instance with the updated location

      • withStatus

        public CalendarEvent withStatus​(EventStatus newStatus)
        Creates a new CalendarEvent with updated status.
        Parameters:
        newStatus - the new status
        Returns:
        a new CalendarEvent instance with the updated status

      • withTimes

        public CalendarEvent withTimes​(java.time.LocalDateTime newStart,
                               java.time.LocalDateTime newEnd)
        Creates a new CalendarEvent with updated start and end times. Useful for rescheduling events.
        Parameters:
        newStart - the new start time
        newEnd - the new end time
        Returns:
        a new CalendarEvent instance with updated times
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if newEnd is before newStart

      • equals

        public boolean equals​(java.lang.Object obj)
        Equality based on (subject, start, end) tuple. Note: This means two events with the same subject and times but different descriptions or locations are considered equal.
        Overrides:
        equals in class java.lang.Object
